steps.title: Find the key claim
steps.content: Núñez and colleagues claim that making temporal distinctions along imagined linear axes is not ubiquitous.

steps.title: Apply the claim to the choices
steps.content: Choice B shows Yupno speakers using the uphill/downhill axis for future gestures, which demonstrates they do not rely on a left/right axis for time. The other choices either describe gesture frequency, hand preference, or shared grammar, none of which address whether the Yupno use a different spatial axis for time. That matches choice B — B is correct.
